title: Ozoroa fulva
intro: Ozoroa fulva, also known as the red stinkwood or yellow stinkwood, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa fulva include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the manufacture of traditional medicines, such as ointments and creams.
taxonomy: Ozoroa fulva is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ozoroa, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "bushpeppertrees" or "milk bush". The species Ozoroa fulva is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Kenya and Tanzania. The classification of Ozoroa fulva is further detailed in the plant's binomial nomenclature, with the combination of the genus name Ozoroa and the species name fulva. This classification helps to identify and distinguish the species from other similar plants in the same genus.
uses: Ozoroa fulva is traditionally used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's bark, roots, and leaves are often used separately or in combination to prepare medicinal remedies. In some regions, the plant is used to treat gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and stomach pain. Its medicinal properties are also attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Further studies are required to confirm the full extent of Ozoroa fulva's medicinal uses and potential applications.

parts: Ozoroa fulva, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its roots, leaves, and bark. preparations: Ozoroa fulva is used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal remedies, with its bark being a primary source of extracts.
